title: P31 Cognitive Passport — v1.1.0 additive overlay
description: Additive overlay schema for p31.cognitivePassport/1.1.0. Documents the optional fields introduced in v1.1.0 on top of the v1.0.0 base. Every v1.0.0-valid document is also v1.1.0-valid. v1.1.0 documents may include any subset of the optional blocks below. The runtime SCHEMA constant in cognitive-passport/index.html and andromeda/04_SOFTWARE/packages/shared/src/cognitive-passport-schema.ts remains 1.0.0 until WCD-PHOS C-4 (generator rebuild) ships and atomically bumps both files. This file is the SPEC; the bump is a separate atomic step.

doctrine.purpose: Make the Cognitive Passport the cryptographic key that unlocks personalization across every P31 surface. v1.0.0 produces the human-readable accommodation summary. v1.1.0 adds machine-readable style + role + AI-context blocks that drive the bus bar (CogPass + PHOS + theme engine + BaseLayout).
doctrine.additivity: All v1.1.0 fields are OPTIONAL. A v1.0.0 document that omits every v1.1.0 block validates against this schema unchanged. The CogPass reader (track B-4) gracefully degrades when a v1.1.0 block is absent.
doctrine.bus_bar_link: identity.accessLevel is the gate that the bus bar (andromeda/04_SOFTWARE/p31ca/ground-truth/p31.ground-truth.json busBar.roles) consumes to decide which navigation slots render.
doctrine.voice_link: communication.preferredTone and stylePreferences.phosRegister together drive the PHOS guide register (warm | technical | minimal). See docs/PHOS-VOICE-DRAFT.md §2.10.
doctrine.personalization_link: stylePreferences.theme/mode and accessibility.* drive window.p31Theme.setTheme()/.setMode() and window.p31SubjectPrefs.set(). The reader is a CONFIGURATOR over the existing engines, not a parallel state store. Reference: andromeda/04_SOFTWARE/p31ca/public/lib/p31-theme-engine.mjs + p31-subject-prefs.js.

$schema.description: When present, declares this passport conforms to the v1.1.0 additive overlay. Absence implies pure v1.0.0.

identity.description: Optional. Self-description that AIs and the bus bar can read. Operator-controlled.

displayName.description: Shown in the BaseLayout nav pill when CogPass is loaded. Optional — pseudonyms welcome.

role.description: Freeform self-description (e.g. 'engineer', 'parent', 'student', 'human'). NOT the access gate — see accessLevel.

oneLiner.description: How you'd describe yourself to an AI in one sentence.

accessLevel.description: Bus bar gate. Default 'user'. The operator sets 'operator' on their own card to surface ops/ede/buffer/glass-box in the nav. This is a self-attestation; not an authentication primitive. True operator-only systems gate via Ed25519 (see k4-agent-hub anchor-pact).

cognition.description: Optional. How this brain works. Read by AI tools as context.

outputBottleneck.description: Freeform: e.g. 'speech', 'writing', 'typing', 'none'. Names the substrate where serialization fails.

executiveFunctionNotes.description: Freeform: what helps, what blocks. Operator-controlled.

communication.description: Optional. How AI tools (and PHOS) should talk to this person.

avoidList.description: Phrases or patterns to never use. Examples: 'As an AI', 'submarine metaphors', 'manufactured urgency'. The operator's avoid list ALWAYS includes naval/military metaphors per .cursorrules §1.

accessibility.description: Optional. Drives window.p31SubjectPrefs.set() on CogPass load. Coordinates with /lib/p31-subject-prefs.js (existing). Mirrors the four canonical axes from docs/P31-DESIGN-DOCTRINE.md §3.

screenComfort.description: Master slider. 0 = strict Gray Rock (no brand chroma, zero motion, even after interaction). 50 = Layer 2 Alive on engagement. 100 = full theme + glass + animations + ambient motion. Reader maps this to motion + animationsEnabled + glassEnabled below.

motionPreference.description: auto = respect prefers-reduced-motion media query. Maps to data-p31-motion attribute via p31SubjectPrefs.

informationDensity.description: Maps to data-p31-density. Six values accept both v6 vocabulary (minimal/standard/dense) and existing p31-subject-prefs vocabulary (comfortable/compact/spacious) for backward compatibility — the reader normalizes.

colorSensitivity.description: Color names or hex strings the user finds uncomfortable. Reader can warn or substitute.

temperaturePreference.description: Maps to data-p31-temp via p31SubjectPrefs (existing axis from p31-subject-prefs.js).

stylePreferences.description: Optional. Drives window.p31Theme.setTheme()/setMode()/setAppearance(). Coordinates with /lib/p31-theme-engine.mjs. The reader is a configurator — no duplicate state.

theme.description: auto = let theme engine pick based on appearance preference + system. Other values map to P31_THEMES keys exactly.

glassEnabled.description: Backdrop-filter glass morphism. False by default per Gray Rock principle. Reader unconditionally sets to false when accessibility.screenComfort < 30.

animationsEnabled.description: Reader unconditionally sets to false when accessibility.screenComfort < 30 OR motionPreference = none/reduced.

phosGuide.description: Show the PHOS guide UI element. False hides the guide (and the absorbed theme switcher, since they are unified per the bus bar 'PHOS absorbs theme switcher' rule).

phosRegister.description: auto resolves from communication.preferredTone: technical → technical, warm → warm, anything else → minimal. PHOS adapts greeting/hint/fallback verbosity per docs/PHOS-VOICE-DRAFT.md §2.10.

context.description: Optional. Pure AI-context block. Used by the 'Copy for Claude / ChatGPT / Gemini' export buttons in the v6 generator (WCD C-4).

currentFocus.description: What you're working on right now.

examples.comment: Pure v1.0.0 document — also valid v1.1.0 (no overlay fields present).
v1.1.0 document — minimal overlay, just access level.
v1.1.0 document — full overlay, operator card.

identity.oneLiner: I think in pictures and build things with code.
